originalAlarm.description: Assigned value is garbage or undefined

sanitizedDescription: Assigned value is garbage or undefined
presenceCondition:
(or (and __STRICT_ANSI__ (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF))
    (and (and __STRICT_ANSI__ ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF)
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME))
    (and (and __STRICT_ANSI__ ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF)
         ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME_REGEXP))
    (and (not __STRICT_ANSI__) (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF))
    (and (and (not __STRICT_ANSI__) ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF)
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME))
    (and (and (not __STRICT_ANSI__) ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF)
         ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME
         (not ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME_REGEXP)))
model:
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_CONF () Bool
  true)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME () Bool
  true)
(define-fun ENABLE_FEATURE_MDEV_RENAME_REGEXP () Bool
  false)
reasoning: At b62bd7b261b.c L14, `char *a = val` assigns a from val. val is initialized at L12 with `val = "mode - device permissions"`. The alarm claiming the assigned value is garbage is a false positive. The PC spans configs where L14 is not even reached (CONF=F or RENAME=F), confirming this is a desugaring artifact.
